title: What is Data Engineering for AI?
summary: Data Engineering for AI is the discipline of designing, building, and operating versioned, observable, cost-efficient data infrastructure specifically optimized for the training, fine-tuning, evaluation, retrieval, and continuous improvement of AI models and agents.
use_when: User asks for the definition, scope, or purpose of Data Engineering for AI.

title: The 2026 AI data stack is PARK + Lance + lakeFS + Feast/Tecton.
summary: The 2026 AI data stack is PARK + Lance + lakeFS + Feast/Tecton.
use_when: User asks a practical or factual question about Data Engineering for AI.

title: 30-70% of frontier model training data is synthetic — your pipeline must treat g
summary: Synthetic data should be treated as a first-class, versioned data artifact; exact real/synthetic ratios must be validated by downstream evaluation.
use_when: User asks a practical or factual question about Data Engineering for AI.

title: Point-in-time correctness is non-negotiable for any time-sensitive feature.
summary: Point-in-time correctness is non-negotiable for any time-sensitive feature.
use_when: User asks a practical or factual question about Data Engineering for AI.

title: Embedding columns must carry model version, hyperparameters, and generation time
summary: Embedding columns must carry model version, hyperparameters, and generation timestamp as metadata.
use_when: User asks a practical or factual question about Data Engineering for AI.

title: lakeFS branching + DVC experiments = the only safe way to run AI data experiment
summary: lakeFS branching + DVC experiments = the only safe way to run AI data experiments at scale.
use_when: User asks a practical or factual question about Data Engineering for AI.

title: Data contracts + LLM validators catch 80%+ of production data issues before they
summary: Data contracts + LLM validators catch 80%+ of production data issues before they reach training.
use_when: User asks a practical or factual question about Data Engineering for AI.

title: Cost per embedding generated is now a first-class metric alongside latency and a
summary: Cost per embedding generated is now a first-class metric alongside latency and accuracy.

title: Multimodal lakehouses (Lance) eliminate the need for separate vector DBs for mos
summary: Multimodal lakehouses (Lance) eliminate the need for separate vector DBs for most workloads.

title: Agent-native patterns require ephemeral scratch spaces + versioned context store
summary: Agent-native patterns require ephemeral scratch spaces + versioned context stores.

title: Governance (provenance, consent, bias) is now a hard requirement for any model t
summary: Governance (provenance, consent, bias) is now a hard requirement for any model that will be deployed to users.

title: If you need Git-like experimentation on petabyte-scale data → lakeFS + DVC
summary: If you need Git-like experimentation on petabyte-scale data → lakeFS + DVC
